title: Pluribus
overview: The most miserable person on Earth must save the world from happiness.
reason: Created by acclaimed showrunner Vince Gilligan for Apple TV, this show boasts an impressive 87 Metacritic score and a high 8.0 IMDb rating from over 127,000 votes, indicating significant anticipation and critical buzz.

title: HIS & HERS
overview: Two estranged spouses — one a detective, the other a news reporter — vie to solve a murder in which each believes the other is a prime suspect.
reason: A Netflix production featuring popular actors Tessa Thompson and Jon Bernthal, it has garnered substantial audience interest with over 65,000 IMDb votes and a respectable 7.2 rating.

title: The Beast in Me
overview: A famous author is pulled into a twisted mind game with her rich, powerful new neighbor — who might be a murderer.
reason: Starring Emmy winners Claire Danes and Matthew Rhys, this Netflix series has attracted significant attention, evidenced by its 7.4 IMDb rating from over 61,000 votes.

title: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms
overview: A century before the events of Game of Thrones, two unlikely heroes wandered Westeros: a young, naive but courageous knight, Ser Duncan the Tall, and his diminutive squire, Egg. Set in an age when the Targaryen line still holds the Iron Throne and the last dragon has not yet passed from living memory, great destinies, powerful foes, and dangerous exploits await these improbable and incomparable friends.
reason: As a highly anticipated Game of Thrones prequel on HBO, created by George R. R. Martin, it carries immense cultural weight and is expected to draw a massive global audience.

title: Wonder Man
overview: Simon and Trevor, two actors at opposite ends of their careers, chase life-changing roles.
reason: Backed by Marvel Studios and Disney+, this series benefits from a massive built-in fanbase and features known actors, achieving a solid 7.6 IMDb rating from over 12,000 votes.
